mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in real retail activity, proxied by rising RSAFS, signals improving household demand and near-term revenue momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot reports RSAFS value available as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month RSAFS growth do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with negative growth over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate tightens discount rates and funding conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the tightening is abrupt.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is available monthly with low revision risk at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, lowering expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot reports UNRATE value available as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases have a nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 return and a hit rate for negative returns below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is rising while the federal funds rate is also rising, equities face both margin pressure and tighter discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month growth is positive and FEDFUNDS rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-up and FEDFUNDS-up state does not underperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 25bp on average.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and payroll employment signals broad real-economy expansion, improving earnings breadth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both increase over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot includes both INDPRO and PAYEMS as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO and PAYEMS 3-month increases fail to deliver positive mean 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A material decline in housing starts signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, lowering cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:latest macro snapshot reports HOUST value available as of t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST weakness is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 45% of cases or has positive average excess returns.

mechanism_narrative: A decelerating CPIAUCSL trend reduces inflation risk and expected policy pressure, supporting valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is available monthly with low revision risk at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if falling 6-month CPIAUCSL growth has lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than rising 6-month CPIAUCSL growth over the validation sample.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains combined with a falling unemployment rate signal improving labor income without visible labor-market stress, supporting risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 1 month and UNRATE falls over 1 month
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state has a 5-day S&P 500 return hit rate below 50% and an average return not above the unconditional mean.
